always.exam: Lumbar screen + neurological screen (myotomes, dermatomes, reflexes, SLR/slump)
always.why: The SIJ is a diagnosis of exclusion — a lumbar source (disc/radiculopathy) must be screened first, since below-knee/dermatomal symptoms point away from the SIJ.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Excludes lumbar radicular source before attributing pain to the SIJ

always.exam: SIJ provocation cluster (thigh thrust, distraction, compression, Gaenslen's, sacral thrust)
always.why: No single SIJ test is diagnostic; Laslett showed 3+ positive of the provocation cluster is ~91% specific for a SIJ pain source.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms/excludes SIJ as the pain source (3+ positive cluster)

always.exam: Inflammatory back pain screen (age of onset, morning stiffness duration, night pain, alternating buttock pain, NSAID response, family history)
always.why: Distinguishes a mechanical SIJ presentation from inflammatory sacroiliitis (axial spondyloarthritis), which needs medical/rheumatology referral rather than mechanical treatment.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens for inflammatory sacroiliitis requiring onward referral

add.exam: Active Straight Leg Raise (ASLR) + symphysis pubis palpation
add.why: Pregnancy-related pelvic girdle pain frequently involves the whole pelvic ring; ASLR grades load-transfer impairment and the symphysis must be assessed alongside the SIJ.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Confirms pelvic girdle pain / load-transfer deficit

add.exam: Refer for MRI SIJ (bone marrow oedema) and HLA-B27 / ESR-CRP bloods
add.why: Alternating buttock pain is a specific inflammatory back pain feature; imaging/serology confirm or exclude axial spondyloarthritis.

supporting.exam: SIJ palpation (PSIS symmetry, long dorsal sacroiliac ligament, sacral sulcus)
supporting.why: Localised SIJ ligament tenderness supports a SIJ source and helps localise the symptomatic side alongside the provocation cluster.

supporting.exam: Hip examination (FADIR, resisted hip external rotation, deep gluteal palpation)
supporting.why: Hip and deep-gluteal sources refer to the buttock and must be differentiated from a true SIJ source.

assessmentLayers.Observation: Pelvic symmetry (PSIS / iliac-crest levels), gait, single-leg-stance pelvic drop
assessmentLayers.Posture: Pelvic tilt / rotation asymmetry, apparent leg-length difference
assessmentLayers.Functional screen: Active straight-leg raise (ASLR load-transfer), Gillet/stork, step-up, single-leg stance
assessmentLayers.STTT (Cyriax): Resisted hip abduction/adduction to differentiate; provocation cluster remains primary
assessmentLayers.CPA (compensation pattern): Gluteus max / medius inhibition vs hamstring / piriformis / adductor overactivity (force closure)
assessmentLayers.Kinetic chain: Hip and lumbar mobility; foot pronation influence on pelvic mechanics
assessmentLayers.Fascia: Posterior oblique sling (glute-max ↔ contralateral lat via TL fascia), long dorsal ligament
